docs.title: Project
docs.text: Website | Github

docs.title: Project Mission
docs.text: To faithfully recreate Myst III: Exile and Myst IV: Revelation.

docs.title: Project Scope
docs.text:
• Myst III: Exile HD (w potential VR mode)
  • Myst IV: Revelation HD (w potential VR mode)
  • Myst III: (full 3d remake in Unreal w VR)
  • Myst IV: (full 3d remake in Unreal w VR)
  • Tomahna For Uru (implementation to have Tomahna as a place visitable in Myst Online)

docs.title: Project Tools
docs.text:
• MYSTER Asset Explorer
  • Scripts for photogrammetry / node alignment
  • Blender Python script for importing nodes
  • AI upscaling / AI performance reconstruction

docs.title: Exile
docs.text: File Reference

docs.title: Install Files
docs.text:
• .m3a
  • .m3s

docs.title: FileMagic string
docs.text: With some exceptions, all asset files have a FileMagic at the start of the file. It can be one of "ubi/b0-l" and "ubi/b0-b", which indicate whether the file is little- or big-endian respectively. However, only "ubi/b0-l", the little-endian variant, has ever been observed in the wild.

docs.title: BasicString structure
docs.text:
The basic UTF-16 string used all over the place. Sometimes referred to as ubistring.

Name Type Description len uint32 Length of the string. s char[len] The string.

docs.title: BasicWString structure
docs.text:
Same as BasicString, except with UTF-16 encoding.

Name Type Description len uint32 Length of the string. s wchar_t[len] The string.

docs.title: EncryptedString structure
docs.text:
Same as BasicString, except all chars are "swizzled". That is to say, for every byte b of s, apply (b & 0xAA) >> 1 | (b & 0x55) << 1 to (un)swizzle.

Name Type Description len uint32 Length of the string. s char[len] The string.

Name Type Description x float32 y float32

docs.title: CompressedQuaternion3U16 structure
CompressedQuaternionU32 type
docs.text:
• Type: uint32
  • Bits: xxaaaaaa aaaabbbb bbbbbbcc cccccccc
    • xx: greatest quat element (0 = x, 1 = y, 2 = z, 3 = w).
    • aaaaaaaaaa/bbbbbbbbbb/cccccccccc: other three elements.

Compression/decompression:

const float32 oneOverRootTwo  = sqrt(2.0) /     2.0;
const float32 scaleRange10Bit = sqrt(2.0) / pow(2.0, 10);

static uint32 CompressedQuaternionU32::CompressElement(float32 elem) {
    return min((uint32)((elem + oneOverRootTwo) * (1.0/scaleRange10Bit) + 0.000025), 0x3FF);
}

void CompressedQuaternionU32::Compress(Quaternion *src) {
    uint32 a, b, c, x;
    float32 ax, ay, az, aw;
    Quaternion q;

    q = *src;
    q.Normalize();

    ax = abs(q.x);
    ay = abs(q.y);
    az = abs(q.z);
    aw = abs(q.w);

    if (ax > ay && ax > az && ax > aw) {
        // x is greatest element.
        if (q.x < 0.0) q.Negate();
        a = CompressElement(q.y);
        b = CompressElement(q.z);
        c = CompressElement(q.w);
        x = 0;
    } else if (ay > az && ay > aw) {
        // y is greatest element.
        if (q.y < 0.0) q.Negate();
        a = CompressElement(q.x);
        b = CompressElement(q.z);
        c = CompressElement(q.w);
        x = 1;
    } else if (az > aw) {
        // z is greatest element.
        if (q.z < 0.0) q.Negate();
        a = CompressElement(q.x);
        b = CompressElement(q.y);
        c = CompressElement(q.w);
        x = 2;
    } else {
        // w is greatest element.
        if (q.w < 0.0) q.Negate();
        a = CompressElement(q.x);
        b = CompressElement(q.y);
        c = CompressElement(q.z);
        x = 3;
    }

    this->v = (x << 30) | (a << 20) | (b << 10) | a;
    return;
}

void CompressedQuaternionU32::Decompress(Quaternion *dst) {
    float32 a, b, c, s;

    a = (float32)(this->v >> 20 & 0x3FF) * scaleRange10Bit - oneOverRootTwo;
    b = (float32)(this->v >> 10 & 0x3FF) * scaleRange10Bit - oneOverRootTwo;
    c = (float32)(this->v >>  0 & 0x3FF) * scaleRange10Bit - oneOverRootTwo;
    s = sqrt(((1.0 - a*a) - b*b) - c*c);

    switch this->v >> 30 {
    case 0:
        dst->x = s;
        dst->y = a;
        dst->z = b;
        dst->w = c;
        break;
    case 1:
        dst->x = a;
        dst->y = s;
        dst->z = b;
        dst->w = c;
        break;
    case 2:
        dst->x = a;
        dst->y = b;
        dst->z = s;
        dst->w = c;
        break;
    case 3:
        dst->x = a;
        dst->y = b;
        dst->z = c;
        dst->w = s;
        break;
    }
    return;
}

docs.title: BigFile
docs.text:
BigFile is the container format employed by Ubisoft for Revelation. All of Revelation's data is contained in BigFiles.

coderjo wrote some Python scripts for manipulating BigFiles.

BigFiles are little-endian, and have the .m4b file extension.

docs.title: BigFile structure
docs.text: Name Type Description magic BasicString Magic signature. Must be "UBI_BF_SIG\0". ver uint32 Version. Must be 1. No other version is known. root FatDirectory Root directory entry. root.name must be empty. fileTbl ... Table of files described in root.

docs.title: FatDirectory structure
docs.text:
Describes a single directory contained in a BigFile.

Name Type Description name BasicString Directory name. In source material, all non-empty directory names have a redundant null-terminator. nSubDirs uint8 Number of sub-directories. subDirs FatDirectory[nSubDirs] Sub-directories. files Array<FatFile> Files.

docs.title: FatFile structure
docs.text:
Describes a single file contained in a BigFile.

Name Type Description name BasicString File name. In source material, all non-empty file names have a redundant null-terminator. size uint32 File size. pos uint32 File's absolute start position in its containing BigFile.

docs.title: ZAP
docs.text:
A container of multiple image files. These are used for transparency layers in static textures. One major example is nodecube textures, where transparency lets the skybox through.

It is unknown what "ZAP" is supposed to stand for, if anything.

ZAP files are little-endian, and have the .zap file extension.

docs.title: ZAP structure
docs.text: Name Type Description hdrSize uint32 Header size. Never observed to be anything other than 0x20. nImages uint32 Number of images. Never observed to be anything other than 0x2. Then again, Revelation is hardcoded to assume all ZAP files contain exactly two images. formats uint32[nImages] Formats of the respective images, as known by the LEADTOOLS SDK, though only three are supported (FILE_JPEG (0xA), FILE_TIF_JPEG (0xB) and FILE_PNG (0x4B)). sizes uint32[nImages] Sizes of the respective image's data. w uint32 Shared width. h uint32 Shared height. data uint8[sizes[i]][nImages] Data of the respective image.

docs.title: Music Sequencing
docs.text:
The music in Myst4 is similar to Myst3 in that it uses sequencing to compose many separate sound files dynamically into music so that the game can offer more variety when listening to the same piece for a long period of time compared to a fixed piece of music looped endlessly.

Each music track / cue is defined in a sequence file (.seq) located within the sequence folder located within the main sound.m4b archive. The game shipped with 48 files of these files (assuming no variation between versions).

docs.title: Sequence Files
docs.text:
This page is a WIP: Do not use it as gospel reference!

Useful Hex Editor designed for Reverse Engineering

Useful Hex Conversion Tool

Endian order: unknown - likely little endian

docs.title: FAMILY Structure
docs.text:
Type Count Description int32 0x1 unknown char ?? Family name int32 0x1 unknown

List of sound names, each preceded by 4 bytes

Type Count Description int32 0x1 unknown char Sequence name char Sequence name

docs.title: SOUND Structure
docs.text: Type Count Description uint 0x1 Sound name length (snl) char snl Sound name short 0x1 Start range short 0x1 End range bool 0x1 Flag int32 0x1 unknown float 0x1 unknown float 0x1 unknown float 0x1 unknown float 0x1 unknown int32 0x1 unknown float 0x1 Vector X float 0x1 Vector Y float 0x1 Vector Z

docs.title: Ubi's Sound Files
docs.text:
This is a post I saved some time ago from a forum that seems to have met it's demise (who says the internet is forever...) The thread was discussing the sound files in Myst 4.

Posted by Simon

Myst IV sound hacking Nov 10, 2004

I just downloaded the Myst IV Revelation demo (it’s free there) and it’s very cool. It brings back memories of Myst and maybe (I can hope) it will be as good as Riven was. Anyway, I guess I’m going to buy it as soon as I’m done writing this, but first, some obligatory info on hacking the sound files out of the demo.

We’re talking about the OS X version here, presumably the PC version is analogous. Go into the .app package and look for sound/data. Ignore the .snd files, they are metadata. Inside “data” you will see files with three different extensions. Here’s my guide to what they are.

LS0 - inside the “English” folder - these are easy, they are straight OGG vorbis, just rename them with the .ogg extension and play in your favorite player.

SS0 - music and some backgrounds. Three of these are straight OGG vorbis files, rename as above. Some of them are ADPCM - see below.

SB0 - many of these are 44100 Hz, 2 channel (stereo), 4-Bit 4:1 IMA DVI ADPCM files (a common format in WAV/wave files). You can listen to them using SoundHack a free program for OS X and there’s probably similar soft for PCs. Open as unknown format. Set the format to the above. Save Info. Save as. The resulting file should be listenable.

NB: Many of these files contain multiple sounds.

NB2: Some of them won’t work using this technique, I don’t know why.

Have fun.

Update: Revelator will extract just the ogg files automatically.

PS: Why all the work? They’re probably just trying to make it difficult to extract the audio by stripping the normal headers from these files. They could have left the WAV headers in place and made it a lot easier.

The ADPCM files seem to be missing the WAV file headers, and the format itself isn't that straight forward to play (with say VLC).

Using something like vgmstream doesn't seem to work with the files as extracted from the game. Wav headers probably need to be programmatically added back onto the files to get them playing properly.

docs.title: thor resource
docs.text: thor is the namespace that houses Revelation's engine implementation. It has its own dedicated resource serialisation scheme.

docs.title: thor resource file structure
docs.text: Name Type Description magic FileMagic Magic signature. res ThorResource Resource.

docs.title: ThorResource structure
docs.text: Name Type Description type ThorResourceType Type. data ... Data according to type.

docs.title: ThorResourceType enum
docs.text:
• Type: uint32

A ThorResourceType identifies the type of thor resource that follows immediately after it.

This enum value table might not be complete!

Name Value Omits header AiResource 0x1 AtlasNodeResource 0x2 BlenderResource 0x3 CommandBlock 0x6 ContainerResource 0x7 Context 0x8 CursorResource 0x9 FlareResource 0xA FontResource 0xB GeometryResource 0xC HotspotResource 0xD Interpolator 0xE TextureBox 0xF LayerExtractResource 0x10 LightResource 0x11 LightmapModAddResource 0x13 LightsetResource 0x14 MaterialResource 0x15 MoreActor 0x16 MoreClip 0x17 MoreModel 0x18 MoreMultiActor 0x19 GraphicOverlay 0x1A PanelResource 0x1B AnimPanelResource 0x1C ParticleSystemBase 0x1D PixelEffectResource 0x1E RailResource 0x1F SkyReflectionResource 0x21 SoundLoader 0x23 Subtitle 0x24 TextResource 0x25 Text 0x26 * TextureResource 0x27 VariableResource 0x28 Video 0x29 WaterResource 0x2A InteractiveOffsetProvider 0x2B TextureAlignedOffsetProvider 0x2C StateData 0x2D WidgetResource 0x2E Widget2D 0x2F Button 0x30 Canvas 0x31 ComboBox 0x32 Label 0x33 ListBox 0x34 ListBoxItem 0x35 PanelWidget 0x36 PanelButton 0x37 Popup 0x38 Credits 0x39 TextWidget 0x3A ToolTips 0x3B ZipWidget 0x3C Slider 0x3D ZPlanesResource 0x3E MultiGeometry 0x40 QualityDatabaseResource 0x41 ShadowMaterialResource 0x42

docs.title: ThorResourceHeader structure
docs.text:
A ThorResourceHeader contains the base info for a thor resource.

Name Type Description ver uint32 Version, indicating which revision of a particular resource type this resource was compiled as. Though there are resource types with multiple versions, Revelation's code appears to ignore this field, always assuming the latest version; some resource headers do not list the latest version. Unlike ubi resource file versions, this version field pertains to just the associated individual resource. name EncryptedString Name. For non-embedded resources, this will be the filename without extension.

docs.title: CommandBlock
docs.text:
• ThorResourceType: 0x6
  • Max. version: 0x1

A CommandBlock contains commands to be executed upon some AI event.

docs.title: CommandBlock structure
docs.text: Name Type Description hdr ThorResourceHeader Header. cmds Array<EncryptedString> Commands.

docs.title: ContainerResource
docs.text:
• ThorResourceType: 0x7
  • Max. version: 0x1

A ContainerResource groups multiple resources together.

docs.title: ContainerResource structure
docs.text: Name Type Description hdr ThorResourceHeader Header. children Array<ThorResource> Children.

docs.title: GeometryResource structure
docs.text: Name Type Description hdr ThorResourceHeader Header. ??? EncryptedString ??? EncryptedString isVisible bool ??? uint32 collisionEventsEnabled bool ??? uint32 layerIndex uint32 ??? bool ??? EncryptedString

docs.title: RailResource
docs.text:
• ThorResourceType: 0x1F
  • Max. version: 0x1

A RailResource describes a rail along which a hotspot can be dragged.

docs.title: RailResource structure
docs.text: Name Type Description hdr ThorResourceHeader Header. hotspotResName EncryptedString Name of the GeometryResource which describes the draggable hotspot for this rail. initialVertex uint16 directionType DirectionType The direction in which the hotspot can be dragged along the rail. sensitivity float32 snapFactor float32 maxAngle float32 isCyclic bool Indicates whether the first and last rail segments connect back to each other, i.e. whether the hotspot can be dragged past the last rail segment to the first (and/or vice versa). orientationX float32 orientationZ float32 vertices Array<RailVertex> Vertices of the rail's cubic Bézier curve. If !isCyclic, vertices[0].in and vertices[vertices.len-1].out are ignored.

docs.title: DirectionType enum
docs.text: Name Value Forward 0x0 Backward 0x1 Bidirectional 0x2

docs.title: RailVertex structure
docs.text:
A RailVertex describes a vertex along a cubic Bézier curve.

Name Type Description pos Vector3 Position. in Vector3 out Vector3

docs.title: Subtitle
docs.text:
• ThorResourceType: 0x24
  • Max. version: 0x1

A Subtitle contains localised subtitles to accompany speech samples.

docs.title: Subtitle structure
docs.text: Name Type Description hdr ThorResourceHeader Header. ??? int32 Revelation's code appears to ignore this field. Never observed to be anything other than 0x1. soundName BasicString Full name of the sound resource to sync to. captions Array<Caption> Captions. In the source material, all captions will have a start time equal to the end time of the caption before it. It is unconfirmed whether this is required.

docs.title: Caption structure
docs.text: Name Type Description start float32 Start time. end float32 End time. text BasicWString Text. Can be empty.

docs.title: Text
docs.text:
• Type: BasicWString
  • ThorResourceType: 0x26
  • Max. version: 0x1

A Text contains an arbitrary UTF-16 string.

docs.title: TextResource
docs.text:
• ThorResourceType: 0x25
  • Max. version: 0x1

A TextResource contains a collection of Text resources, possibly nested in one or more child TextResources.

docs.title: TextResource structure
docs.text: Name Type Description hdr ThorResourceHeader Header. ??? int32 Revelation's code appears to ignore this field. Never observed to be anything other than 0x1. blip Blip Root blip.

docs.title: Blip structure
docs.text:
Under the hood a Blip is really just a TextResource without the header and unknown int32.

Name Type Description nTexts int32 Number of texts. nGroups int32 Number of groups. texts BlipText[nTexts] Texts. groups BlipGroup[nGroups] Groups.

docs.title: BlipText structure
docs.text: Name Type Description resName BasicString Text resource name. text Text Child text.

docs.title: BlipGroup structure
docs.text: Name Type Description resName BasicString TextResource resource name. group Blip Child group.

• ThorResourceType: 0x27
  • Max. version: 0x2

A TextureResource contains a single texture image, or a reference to one if it is localised.

docs.title: TextureResource structure
docs.text: Name Type Condition Description hdr ThorResourceHeader Header. isLocalised bool Indicator of whether this texture has different localised versions. path BasicString isLocalised Virtual path to localised texture file. Never observed to refer to anything but raw PNG files. data ImageData !isLocalised Image data.

docs.title: ImageData structure
docs.text: Name Type Description format EncryptedString Format of data. Never observed to be anything other than "png". data Array<uint8> Data.

docs.title: VariableResource
docs.text:
• ThorResourceType: 0x28
  • Max. version: 0x1

A VariableResource contains the value of a variable.

docs.title: VariableResource structure
docs.text: Name Type Condition Description hdr ThorResourceHeader Header. type uint32 Type. v uint8 type == 0x1 Value. v uint16 type == 0x2 Value. v uint32 type == 0x3 Value. v int8 type == 0x4 Value. v int16 type == 0x5 Value. v int32 type == 0x6 Value. v float32 type == 0x7 Value. v float64 type == 0x8 Value. v bool type == 0x9 Value. v BasicString type == 0xA Value. v Vector3 type == 0xB Value.

docs.title: Video
docs.text:
• ThorResourceType: 0x29
  • Max. version: 0x5

A Video describes the properties and location of a Bink (.bik) video file.

docs.title: Video structure
docs.text: Name Type Condition Description hdr ThorResourceHeader Header. ??? bool Revelation's code appears to ignore this field. path EncryptedString Virtual path to video file. hasAlpha bool Whether the video contains transparency data. preload bool Whether the video should be preloaded. isMonochrome bool Whether the video is monochrome. dataRate uint32 hasKeyframes bool Whether this resource contains keyframes for easy seeking. keyframes ThorResource hasKeyframes ContainerResource called "keyframe" containing VariableResources with uint32 type. Resource is ignored if not a ContainerResource, and contained resources are ignored if not VariableResource. However, VariableResources must have uint32 type, or Revelation will crash.

docs.title: ubi resource file structure
docs.text: Name Type Description magic FileMagic Magic signature. ver uint32 Global version. Individual resource types can have different binary contents depending on this version. res UbiResource Resource.

docs.title: ares::Texture
docs.text:
• UbiResourceType: "ares::Texture" and "ares::TextureFrame"

It is unknown what the distinction is between ares::Texture and ares::TextureFrame; they share the same serialization routine.

docs.title: ares_tools::Font
docs.text:
• UbiResourceType: "ares_tools::Font"

An ares_tools::Font describes a font. They are contained exclusively in .fnt files found in data.m4b/common/common.m4b/font.

docs.title: ares_tools::Font structure
docs.text: Name Type Description name BasicString Instance name. nTextures uint32 Number of .tga texture files containing the font's letters. They are in the same directory as the .fnt file with a one-indexed _%d suffix in the filename. letters Array<Letter> Letters.

docs.title: Letter structure
docs.text:
Name Type Description code uint16 Code point. lowerLeftTexCoord Vector2 upperRightTexCoord Vector2 textureIndex int Index of texture file containing this letter.

The floats in both tex coords consider the bottom-left (0.0, 0.0), and the upper right (1.0, 1.0).

docs.title: Commands
docs.text:
• mkdocs new [dir-name] - Create a new project.
  • mkdocs serve - Start the live-reloading docs server.
  • mkdocs build - Build the documentation site.
  • mkdocs -h - Print help message and exit.

docs.title: Project layout
docs.text:
mkdocs.yml    # The configuration file.
docs/
    index.md  # The documentation homepage.
    ...       # Other markdown pages, images and other files.

docs.text:
A plain codeblock:

Some code here

docs.title: Code for a specific language
docs.text:
Some more code with the py at the start:

import tensorflow as tf
def whatever()

docs.title: With a title
With line numbers
docs.text:
def bubble_sort(items):
    for i in range(len(items)):
        for j in range(len(items) - 1 - i):
            if items[j] > items[j + 1]:
                items[j], items[j + 1] = items[j + 1], items[j]

docs.title: Highlighting lines
docs.text:
def bubble_sort(items):
    for i in range(len(items)):
        for j in range(len(items) - 1 - i):
            if items[j] > items[j + 1]:
                items[j], items[j + 1] = items[j + 1], items[j]
